Project Manager II
description:
**Assiniboine Credit Union is hiring for the position of a permanent Full\-Time Project Manager II to work at the PMO \& Integration department.**


**About this opportunity:**


* This role requires to work 37\.5 hours per week, Monday to Friday.
* This role will be an hybrid position: some days working from home, and other days at the Head Office (540 St Anne's Rd, Winnipeg MB, R2M 5R7\).
* The position offers a competitive compensation plan, including health and dental benefits, matched pension, and 3 weeks of vacation.

  



Reporting to the Manager, Project Management, Project Manager II is a senior project management professional responsible for leading large\-scale, complex, and high\-impact initiatives that advances ACU’s strategic priorities. This role owns the delivery of multi\-stream projects with significant organizational risk, complexity, and interdependencies, requiring advanced judgment, autonomy, and stakeholder influence.



Project Manager II acts as a trusted advisor to leaders, enforces and evolves PMO standards, and mentors Project Manager I peers. Drawing on deep experience and PMI/PMBOK best practices, this role ensures disciplined delivery, strong governance, and sustained business outcomes aligned with Assiniboine Credit Union/ Caisse Assiniboine (ACU)’s commitment to rural and agricultural communities, francophone services, values\-based banking and ACU’s triple bottom line accountability to people, planet, and prosperity.

  


**Responsibilities:**

**Project Management:**

* Lead the end\-to\-end delivery of complex, cross\-functional projects, ensuring alignment with approved scope, schedule, budget, risk, quality, and dependencies.
* Own and manage project planning, governance, and delivery execution, exercising independent judgment to resolve issues and escalating only material risks.
* Provide executive\-level project reporting and decision support, including status, risk, change impacts, and recommendations.
* Lead cross\-functional project teams, fostering accountability, collaboration, and high performance.
* Ensure effective risk, issue, and quality management, including smooth transitions to operations, and measurement of post\-implementation outcomes and benefits realization.
* Oversee third\-party vendors and contractors; supporting contract negotiations, and ensure delivery and contractual compliance.
* Ensure all project documentation meets PMO standards and audit requirement; capture lessons learned to strengthen delivery practices.


**Business Analysis \& Stakeholder Engagement:**


* Act as a trusted advisor to business units, IT/Digital teams, and vendors to support informed decision making and solution delivery.
* Support complex business process analysis and redesign initiatives
* Oversee the translation of business needs into functional requirements and manage scope and changes throughout the solution lifecycle.
* Support build\-versus\-buy assessments and oversee user acceptance testing (UAT) to ensure solutions meet business needs
* Lead stakeholder engagement activities, including workshops and interviews to identify opportunities and drive effective change adoption.


**Continuous Improvement**


* Lead continuous improvement initiatives to enhance PMO processes, tools, and delivery practices.
* Drive the consistent adoption and evolution of project management standards across the organization.
* Mentor and coach Project Manager I peers, supporting PMO capability development and maturity.
* Apply lessons learned to improve future project delivery outcomes and overall value realization.

  


**EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE**



The competencies for the position would typically be acquired through 5\-7\+ years of progressive project management experience and a related post\-secondary degree or diploma, or an equivalent combination of education and experience. A Project Management Professional (PMP) designation is required.



Business Analysis certification (CBAP) and Organizational Change Management training are considered strong assets. Practical and operational business experience and knowledge within the financial services sector is required. Additional formal certifications are preferred.


**Key Occupational Skills**


* Strong understanding and application of PMBOK practices within regulated environments
* Ability to lead complex, multi\-stream projects, managing scope, schedule, budget, risk, quality, and dependencies
* Strong portfolio\-level judgment, including prioritization, sequencing, and trade\-off analysis
* Proven ability to lead and influence cross\-functional teams and senior stakeholders without direct authority
* Advanced stakeholder engagement and executive\-level communication skills
* Understanding of technological trends and the ability to apply them to delivery effective business solutions
* Ability to translate business needs into functional requirements and oversee solution validation and user acceptance testing (UAT)
* Strong analytical and systems thinking skills, with experience in business process redesign and solution enablement
* Demonstrated coaching and mentoring capability to support PMO maturity and peer development
* Experience managing third‑party vendors, including contract and performance oversight
* Advanced knowledge in quality control and assurance processes, particularly related to testing leading and facilitation
* Strong continuous\-improvement mindset, with the ability to strengthen project delivery standards, tools, and practices

  


**WHO WE ARE?**



As a Certified B Corporation® and one of Manitoba’s Top Employers (2024\), the culture at Assiniboine Credit Union / Caisse Assiniboine is supportive of the individual while focused on the greater community. With the widest geographic reach of any credit union in Manitoba, you’ll be motivated by shared values and encouraged to always think about how, through financial sustainability, we can do more for our members, our employees and our community.



Choosing to work at Assiniboine Credit Union means that you’ll be working for a uniquely Manitoban credit union that:


* puts people, planet and prosperity first,
* values diversity and inclusion,
* is committed to serving its urban, rural, agricultural, and Francophone communities,
* is focused on social and environmental responsibility,
* supports employees through training programs and long\-term career development,
* offers a competitive total compensation program which includes bonuses, a matched pension program, and health and dental benefits.


If doing things differently and doing more is part of who you are, join us on a rewarding journey where your personal aspirations and career goals can align.

  

**If you are interested in applying for the Project Manager II role, please submit your application and updated resume by Friday, April 10th, 2026, 4:00 pm.** We thank everyone who applies, but only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.  

  

*We are committed to workforce diversity and actively recruit people with diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives reflecting the community in which we live and work. Applicants may request reasonable accommodation related to the materials and activities used throughout the selection process.*

description:
**Project Accounting Analyst**


**Winnipeg, MB**  

**Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers!** We are a leader among energy companies in  

North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's  

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.


***Great Benefits***


* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
* Defined\-benefit pension plan for long\-term financial security.
* Nine\-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and



community.


***Position Overview:***



Under the general direction of the Generation Projects Financial Services Lead will be primarily responsible for providing project  

financial support on various construction projects managed by the Generation Project Management Department (GPD). This will  

include responsibility for providing support in monitoring and controlling expenditures and reviewing and processing  

invoices/progress statements.


***Responsibilities:***


* Review, analyze and/or prepare Invoices, Progress Statements, Miscellaneous Billing Requests and verify that payment


claims are valid and in compliance with the Terms and Conditions of payment. Evaluate/substantiate the accuracy and validity  

of backup documentation and make recommendations to Project Managers.

and progress statements for various contracts. Obtain appropriate signing authority with respect to release/approval.

of financial transactions, working with SAP cost reports and processing/parking journal entries.

inquiries/requests for additional information or analysis.

sent to Accounts Payable section to ensure supplier cheque receipt. Follow up and respond to late payment inquiries.  

* Provide training and guidance on the use of SAP for purchase requisition, service entries and project expenditure reports.
* Respond to internal and external customer inquiries with respect to contract progress claims, back\-up verification and


payments.

Payable and suppliers/contractors to expedite solutions to problems and respond to telephone and written inquiries related to  

the status of progress claims/payments.

role;

title: PROJECT ENGINEER
description:
**Project Engineer**


**Winnipeg, MB**  

**Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers!** We are a leader among energy companies in  

North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's  

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.


***Great Benefits***


* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
* Defined\-benefit pension plan for long\-term financial security.
* Nine\-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and



community.


***Position Overview:***



We are seeking a Project Engineer to join our Generations Project Department. Under the guidance of the Dispatchable Capacity  

Project Manager, you will take a lead role in delivering high impact projects from concept to completion. You'll supervise and  

motivate a cross functional team while providing end to end project management across initiation, planning, execution, and close out  

phases.



In this role, you'll collaborate with multidisciplinary stakeholders to define project scope, develop realistic budgets and schedules,  

assess risk, and establish quality standards. You'll also shape resourcing and procurement strategies, and actively manage  

consultants, suppliers, contractors, and internal teams to ensure successful outcomes. This is an opportunity to drive complex  

projects forward, influence results, and contribute to meaningful, organization wide initiatives.


***Responsibilities:***


* Provide input or lead the development of project deliverables such as schedules, specifications, risk registers, cost estimates,


environmental and safety initiatives, etc.
* Responsible for coordination between the Project Management team, and multi\-disciplinary internal and external technical


groups, including planning, design, maintenance \& operations, engineering consultants and/or contractors.
* May supervise and mentor project staff including, staff officers, project specialists and Engineers.
* Lead, and provide input to, the development of CIJ (Capital Investment Justification) and CIJ Addendums including scope,



schedule, risk and cost estimates.  

* Provide project management expertise to support project participants regarding interdisciplinary engineering, financial, and


resource issues, and in consultation with stakeholders, establish project safety and environmental plans.  

* Lead, coordinate and/or participate in the management of Engineering consultants and/or contractors.
* Initiate, coordinate and direct the preparation of construction and supply tender documents, evaluations and


recommendations for contract award.  

* Ensure adequate performance information is provided to monitor, control and communicate critical issues and deadlines and


manage accountability with all Project Participants and stakeholders.  

* Responsible for the procurement, administration and close\-out for construction, supply and service contracts.
* Required to perform on\-site management of construction and commissioning activities in relief of the site Construction


Engineer or Contract Administrator occasionally.


***Qualifications:***


* Graduate in Engineering from a University of recognized standing, with a minimum of seven years related experience, of


which three years' experience must have been in project management, including three years of supervisory experience.
* Professional member in good standing with Engineers Geoscientists Manitoba (or willingness and ability to attain within a


specified amount of time).

MANITOBA HYDRO IS COMMITTED TO DIVERSITY AND EMPLOYMENT EQUITY


 Reference Code: CO57181152\-01


* Experience related to thermal generating facilities and formal project management training/education is an asset.
* Experience leading a team in delivering large capital projects, requiring specialized project management and planning is



considered an asset.  

* Experience in contract models for mega projects (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction/ Management,


Cost\-Reimbursable, Design Build, Construction Manager at Risk) for Large Projects is considered an asset.  

* Experience directing internal and external design and/or construction teams.
* Possess effective organizational and leadership abilities.
* Good computer skills including experience with scheduling software and the Microsoft Office suite of software.
* Strong organizational and leadership abilities.
* Mature judgment and proven ability to carry out assigned duties with a minimum of direction and supervision.
* Demonstrated high degree of initiative and ability to build positive and effective working relationships with others at all levels,


both inside and outside the corporation.  

* Must obtain and maintain a current Personal Risk Assessment and a "Clear" security rating in accordance with Manitoba


Hydro policy P513\.  

* Possess and maintain a valid Province of Manitoba Driver's Licence.

***Salary Range***

title: Intermediate Civil/Municipal Project Engineer
description:
Your Employer:
**Your mission**


You will play an important role in a dynamic and passionate team, collaborating on a variety of projects of all sizes and scopes in the environment, renewable energies, hydroelectricity, transportation, materials, buildings and climate change markets. Boldness, innovation and collaboration are an integral part of our daily operations.

**Englobe \- Who we are**


At Englobe, our rigor is matched by sincere care for our colleagues, our customers, our communities, and the environment. We are committed to making a real difference, one project at a time, and to having a positive, lasting impact. We approach our work with fresh perspectives, relying on collaboration and a diversity of voices. With an entrepreneurial spirit, we empower our customers and communities with boldness and optimism. We are determined to leave a lasting legacy for generations to come.

**MPE** is seeking **Civil/** **Municipal Project Engineer** to join our team in our **Winnipeg office.**
The successful candidates will play a key role in the implementation of Municipal Infrastructure projects and must have a proven track record in project management within civil engineering and the ability to lead projects from conception to completion.  

Does this sound like the right fit for you? Learn more about the role !


Your Contribution: **Your main responsibilities*** Manage and oversee civil/municipal projects, ensuring timely and cost\-effective delivery;
* Assist with preliminary and detailed design, construction observation and contract administration;
* Lead project teams and coordinate with clients, contractors, and stakeholders;
* Participate in client and stakeholder meetings and resolve any outstanding design considerations or infrastructure challenges;
* Lead projects that actually shape communities—roads people drive on, systems people rely on, and infrastructure that keeps cities running
* Administer construction contracts and support the project team through construction and commissioning;
* Ensure compliance with regulatory standards and company quality guidelines.
* Apply and promote health and safety rules and comply with Englobe's quality procedures.


Your Profile: **What makes you unique*** Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering
* Be registered as a Professional engineer with APEGM
* Must have between 5 – 10 years of experience in civil/municipal project engineering
* Strong leadership and communication skills
* Proven ability to manage multiple projects and deliver results on time
* Familiarity with local municipal standards and regulations
* Computer software knowledge in modelling and spreadsheet analysis;
* Show an aptitude for general construction methods;
* Possess strong communication skills, both verbal and written
* Apply all necessary health and safety procedures
